settings.title: Ten Thousand Loaves for the Food Pantry
settings.excerpt: Every unsold loaf goes somewhere good, and this month we crossed a number we are proud of.
settings.body:
From the first week Crumbly opened, we made a rule that bread does not go in the bin. Whatever does not sell goes to the food pantry two streets over.

This month their coordinator told us the running total had passed ten thousand loaves since we started. We had honestly lost count.

How the rule started

It began as common sense rather than a grand plan. We were a new bakery throwing out perfectly good bread each evening, and the pantry two streets over was short of exactly that. So we started carrying a few crates over at close. It took almost no effort, and it solved two problems at once. Over the years those few crates a day quietly added up to a number none of us had been tracking.

The bread goes out the same evening it is baked, so what arrives is still good rather than something on its way to stale. The pantry sorts it alongside their other donations and it reaches people that week.

How it works in practice

  • Unsold loaves are set aside at close, not the next morning.
  • We drop them at the pantry two streets over the same evening.
  • It costs us only a few minutes and keeps good bread out of the bin.

It costs us nothing but a few minutes at close, and it means someone eats well tonight. If your group could use a standing donation, come talk to us. We would rather give it away than waste it.
